steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Rewrite by distributing the minus sign to every term in the second polynomial:
$$(4x^3 - 5x^2 + 3) - (6x^3 + 2x^2 - x)$$
$$= 4x^3 - 5x^2 + 3 - 6x^3 - 2x^2 + x$$

steps.title: Combine like terms by degree
steps.content:
Group terms by their power of $x$:
  • $x^3$ terms: $4x^3 - 6x^3 = -2x^3$
  • $x^2$ terms: $-5x^2 - 2x^2 = -7x^2$
  • $x$ terms: $+x$
  • constants: $+3$
$$-2x^3 - 7x^2 + x + 3$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $-10x^3 - 3x^2 + x + 3$ — the $x^3$ coefficient would require $4+(-6)=-10$, but subtraction gives $-2$, not $-10$. Eliminated.
  • B: $-2x^3 - 7x^2 + x + 3$ — matches $-2x^3$, $-7x^2$, $+x$, $+3$ exactly.
  • C: $-2x^3 - 3x^2 + x + 3$ — $x^2$ coefficient is $-3$ instead of the correct $-7$. Eliminated.
  • D: $10x^3 - 7x^2 - x + 3$ — wrong sign on both $x^3$ and $x$. Eliminated.
That confirms B is correct.
